extra.reason: Closed

Fixed in this commit: pass filename_format='{instrument}.{codec}' to Spleeter.separate_to_file so output lands at <output_dir>/accompaniment.wav matching _instrumental_path. Test added: test_separate_vocals_writes_to_output_dir_root.
Fixed: pause intent now transitions to PAUSED state when player was playing, keeping state in sync with physical player and allowing wakeword to reactivate.
Fixed via TDD: extracted _on_wakeword_detected helper, removed duplicate _clip_buffer append. Added regression test tests/test_wakeword_detection_clip.py.
Fixed: _show_idle_once now tracks idle kind and replaces stale black mpv proc; wake_screen and hourly cycle restore image/refresh after blank
Fixed: cache by model_name dict instead of single global. Tests added in tests/test_transcribe.py

Fixed: pre-allocated SchroederReverb scratch buffers (comb_sum, scaled_sig, result_f64, result_f32) and JackAudioEngine downsample scratch (_ds_scratch, _ds_f32_scratch, _wet_scratch). Replaced np.append-based _ds_buf with fixed scratch + leftover counter. Replaced per-callback int16 allocation and consumer-side np.concatenate with a pre-allocated ring buffer (_ring, _ring_write, _ring_read). tracemalloc verifies zero audio.py allocations across 50 steady-state callbacks; remaining allocations are scipy.signal.lfilter internals, which the acceptance criteria explicitly permit.

Fixed: _wait_for_end now gates on_end_callback on 'self._proc is proc'. When _start_mpv replaces a running song, the old wait thread sees its proc is no longer the active one and skips firing the callback - so the queue does not advance past the newly-started song. Tests verify both the replacement path (no callback) and natural-end path (callback fires).

Fixed: Player tracks _idle_sub_path; _kill_idle_proc unlinks it when the proc dies, and _show_idle_once unlinks any prior path before writing a new one. /tmp no longer accumulates auto-kj-idle-*.ass files across hourly idle/blank cycles.

Fixed: SongPipeline tracks in-flight requests by normalized name (pre-search) and by youtube_id (post-search). Concurrent duplicate requests for the same song now short-circuit, preventing duplicate downloads, separations, and queue entries. Tests verify both the pre-search name dedup (back-to-back identical and casing/whitespace variants) and that completed requests release the dedup slot.

Fixed: applied a 4th-order Butterworth lowpass at 7.5kHz (Wn=0.3125 of 24kHz Nyquist) to the 48kHz mic stream before the existing 3:1 decimation. Filter state is carried across JACK callbacks via lfilter zi. Tests verify 12kHz content is attenuated to <0.05 RMS while 1kHz content passes with minimal attenuation. The added lfilter call is the only new allocation; tracemalloc confirms audio.py itself remains effectively allocation-free in steady state.

Closed
Added encoding='utf-8' to save_lrc open() call; added regression test verifying the explicit kwarg

synth() now parses WAV via wave.open() to handle extra chunks, returns actual sample rate, and play() uses it. Added 4 tests covering espeak header parsing, extra chunks, piper preference, and play resampling.
Added tests/test_speak.py with coverage for synth() and play(), mocking jack/subprocess. Covers stock header, extra chunks, piper preference, and play resampling per auto-kj-u3d parity.
Switched _save_clip timestamp from %H%M%S to %H%M%S_%f (microsecond precision). Added regression test that calls _save_clip twice without sleep and asserts two files.

Split stop() into public stop() (acquires _lock) and _stop_locked() (caller holds lock). _start_mpv() now calls _stop_locked. Added regression tests verifying lock is held during terminate and that concurrent stop/_wait_for_end never double-fires the on_end callback.

Pre-allocate the partial-tail pad scratch outside the JACK RT callback (lazy on first partial, reused thereafter). Added test_play_buffer_tts_callback_does_not_allocate_per_call verifying at most 1 np.zeros call across 10 partial callbacks.

Replaced nearest-neighbor integer-index resample in audio.py play_buffer and speak.py play() with scipy.signal.resample_poly. Anti-alias filter rolls off above source Nyquist; verified by test driving a 8kHz tone and confirming >30dB attenuation of imaging above 12kHz.

Replaced 60s polling loop with event-driven SongQueue.on_add callback. _on_queue_song_added starts playback immediately when state==IDLE. _try_start_playback retained as thin compat shim that delegates to the same handler. Added tests for the callback wiring and for non-interruption while PLAYING.
